name: Gwyn ap Nudd
description: Gwyn ap Nudd is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology, often depicted as the king of the Tylwyth Teg, the fairy folk. He is a complex and multifaceted deity, associated with the Wild Hunt, the underworld, and the souls of the dead. His origins are somewhat obscure, but he is believed to be an ancient Celtic god whose worship evolved over time. Gwyn is a powerful warrior and hunter, leading his spectral hounds across the night sky, pursuing lost souls or engaging in eternal battles. He is also linked to Annwn, the Welsh Otherworld, where he may rule or at least have significant influence. He is often described as a dark and formidable figure, but also possesses aspects of a protector or guide for those who have passed on. His domain extends to the natural world, particularly the forests and mountains, and he is seen as a guardian of the wild creatures that dwell there. Encounters with Gwyn ap Nudd are often perilous, and those who cross his path may find themselves drawn into the mysteries and dangers of the Otherworld. He is a figure of both fear and respect in Welsh folklore, embodying the untamed forces of nature and the mysteries of life and death. He is often seen as a psychopomp, guiding souls to the afterlife, and is associated with the festivals of Samhain and Yule. His power is tied to the land and the seasons, making him a vital figure in the cycle of life and death.
subtitle: King of the Fairies and the Hunt

keyMyths.title: The Battle of the Trees
keyMyths.description: Gwyn ap Nudd is mentioned in the Cad Goddeu (Battle of the Trees) as a powerful warrior. His presence indicates his association with magic and conflict.

keyMyths.title: Gwyn and Gwythyr
keyMyths.description: Gwyn's eternal battle with Gwythyr ap Greidol for the hand of Creiddylad is a central myth. They fight every May Day until Doomsday.

keyMyths.title: Leading the Wild Hunt
keyMyths.description: Gwyn leads the Wild Hunt, a spectral procession of hunters and hounds. This hunt is often seen as an omen of death or disaster.
